summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting to discuss repair versus replacement options for Station 18 Firehouse, which was damaged by storms and has pre-existing structural deficiencies dating back to its 1982 construction. Chief Myers presented renovation costs of approximately $2.3 million and new construction estimates of approximately $6 million, with potential funding from Ohio EMA (75% of the insurance gap), a state bill (HB 244) for an additional $2.5 million, and BRIC grants. The Board expressed a desire to build a new code-compliant firehouse without raising taxes and decided to continue deliberation at the next regular trustee meeting.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
Motion to adjourn the meeting passed unanimously
action_items: Board to place Station 18 firehouse discussion on the next regular trustee meeting agenda
Staff to seek clarification on whether Ohio EMA 75% funding applies to renovation as well as new construction
Staff to pursue BRIC (Building Resilient Infrastructure and Communities) grant application
Monitor progress of House Bill 244 introduced by Senator Johnson and Representative Jean Schmidt for additional $2.5 million in funding
Chief Myers and staff to continue gathering answers to outstanding questions before next meeting
key_amounts: $2,259,000 - estimated cost to repair storm damage at Station 18
$6,000,000 - estimated cost for new building construction
~$4 million - estimated funding gap between insurance and new build cost
~$3 million - Ohio EMA 75% coverage of the funding gap
$2.5 million - amount in House Bill 244 for additional state funding
$22 million - federal funding threshold for uninsured claims (not met)
$1 million - amount the township could squeeze into budget for a mortgage if needed
~$1.5 million - potential surplus if HB 244 passes, to cover soft costs
notable_topics: Station 18 Firehouse repair vs. replacement options
Structural deficiencies of 1982 building including walls not connected to foundation
Building code compliance for essential/category 4 buildings (250 mph wind zone)
House Bill 244 for additional $2.5 million state funding
BRIC grant application for resilient infrastructure
Goal of zero additional tax burden on residents
Public comment on urgency and fiscal responsibility
Social media misinformation about insurance coverage
attendees: Steve Pegram (public speaker, former fire department member)
notes: The transcript has significant portions of garbled/corrupted text likely due to automated captioning errors. The station was damaged approximately 14-16 months prior to this meeting. The meeting lasted approximately 46 minutes (6:00 PM to 6:46 PM). Trustee Lisa Hodges expressed concern about unanswered questions and timeline for EMA funding. The board emphasized wanting to avoid any new taxes or levies for the project.
title: BOT Special Meeting 9-19-23
